title: LIBS — Laser-Induced Breakdown Spectroscopy imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Cremers-Radziemski 2013 Handbook of LIBS; Motto-Ros et al. 2012 LIBS imaging; Hahn-Omenetto 2012 part II review.

E.description: A pulsed laser (typically Nd:YAG, 1064 or 266 nm, ns pulse) is focused onto a sample, ablating a ~micrograms of material and creating a plasma at T~10000 K. As the plasma cools (1-100 us gate), excited atoms emit characteristic lines; a spectrograph + gated ICCD records I(lambda, r) across a raster map; calibration relates peak intensity to element concentration.
E.forward_model: y(r, lambda) = sum_k A_k * g_k * exp(-E_k / k_B T(r)) * c_k(r) * instrument_response(lambda) + continuum(r, lambda) + n
E.world_state_x: Elemental concentrations c_k(r) per pixel (Z species)
E.observation_y: LIBS hyperspectral cube y in R^{H x W x N_lambda}
physical_parameters_theta: Delay and gate width (100 ns - 100 us)
Plasma temperature T and electron density n_e
Self-absorption (strong for Fe, Al major elements)

W.uniqueness: calibration-free LIBS (CF-LIBS) under LTE assumption; univariate regression for major elements
W.regime: Plasma physics non-linear but under LTE the Saha-Boltzmann equation yields explicit calibration; self-absorption violates linearity; matrix-effect mismatch dominant uncertainty.

C.solver_class: Univariate linear regression, PLS, MCR-ALS, CF-LIBS, random-forest / SVM classification, learned (CNN-LIBS, LIBSNet)
C.error_bound: Concentration NMSE bounded by plasma-temperature fluctuation sigma_T^2 * (E_k/k_B T)^2; CF-LIBS ~5-10% LOD
C.complexity: Linear reg O(H*W*K*N_lambda); PLS O(iter*K*H*W*N_lambda); CF-LIBS O(H*W*K^2)
